decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs timed phylogeographic analysis of whole-genome data to reconstruct the worldwide spread and transmission events of E. coli O157:H7.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen phylogeography application to international E. coli O157:H7 transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeography alongside population genetics to infer gene flow and dispersal of Pratylenchus capsici.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Application concerns nematode population history rather than an infectious-pathogen lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes the phylogeography and regional transmission history of infectious hematopoietic necrosis virus in China using sequence-based coalescent phylogenetics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic transmission and evolution of a fish virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces the Fréchet tree distance for comparing phylogeographic spread paths across trees and assessing reconstruction quality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological-development paper defining a new distance measure for phylogeographic comparisons.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic approaches to relate West Nile virus circulation to terrestrial-bird migration routes in the United States.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of phylogeography to zoonotic-virus circulation and surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeography on H5N1 and H7N9 sequences to estimate migration patterns and spatial-diffusion predictors in China.
Uses whole-genome phylogenetic reconstruction and geographic lineage distributions to characterize the phylogeography of multidrug-resistant S. Typhi in sub-Saharan Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic investigation of pathogen distributions across African regions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic analysis of influenza sequences to infer origins, migrations, and regional reseeding patterns.
Presents AQUAPONY, a web tool for interactively visualizing ancestral geographic annotations and alternative phylogeographic scenarios on trees.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation supporting visualization and interpretation of phylogeographic results.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Re-analyzes the phylogeography of the Triatoma dimidiata complex to infer its origin and haplogroup divergence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The focal phylogeographic application concerns vector population history, not pathogen diffusion itself.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeography and Bayesian phylogenetic inference to reconstruct HIV-1 migration between Ugandan fishing and general-population communities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV transmission and migration application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses previously established phylogeographic population structure with whole-genome sequencing to assign likely local acquisition of coccidioidomycosis cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete use of pathogen phylogeographic structure for infection-source attribution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports sequence-typed clades and their geographic distributions, but the abstract does not describe phylogeographic inference or genomic-diffusion reconstruction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit methodological, diagnostic, implementation, guidance, or concrete phylogeographic-analysis lead is established.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a framework that analytically incorporates uncertain sampling locations into discrete virus phylogeography and evaluates it against reference scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The new uncertainty framework is explicitly implemented in BEAST v1.10.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to sequence, location, and date data to investigate PEDV dissemination among Taiwanese farms.
Uses Bayesian discrete phylogeographic inference to reconstruct international and within-Spain HCV transmission patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis C virus seeding and dispersal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines viral genomes, epidemiologic interviews, and rodent locations in phylogeographic analyses that trace likely Hantaan-virus infection sites.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete source-tracing application for human HFRS infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic analysis of complete HBV genomes to infer the origin and regional spread of subgenotype F4.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis B virus migration reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates hantavirus diversification and phylogeographic structure to infer geographic origins and host-switching spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to zoonotic hantavirus evolutionary geography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs Bayesian phylogeographic analysis and ancestral-state reconstruction on a major HIV-1 CRF02_AG cluster in Spain.
Applies discrete and continuous-space diffusion models to complete Zika-virus genomes to reconstruct origins and spread.
Uses full-genome phylogeny and geographic spread patterns to characterize the phylogeographic history of equine influenza lineages in Asia.
Uses phylogeographic and Bayesian phylodynamic analyses to characterize regional clustering and transboundary dispersal of H5 avian influenza viruses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete avian-influenza evolution and dispersal application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic analysis of rabies-virus sequences to infer spatial movement and geographic constraints in China.
Uses MLVA and whole-genome SNP data to identify phylogeographic patterns and track Brucella suis spread among wildlife and swine.
This perspective advocates sequencing and phylogeography in epidemiology but does not introduce, apply, critique, diagnose, correct, or implement a specific phylogeographic method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit perspective offering guidance on incorporating sequencing and phylogeographic source attribution into public-health practice.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses genetic and epidemiological data to analyze rubella-virus phylogeography, regional persistence, and source-sink structure in Asia.
Applies Bayesian phylogeographic and phylodynamic analyses to infer the origin and diffusion routes of lineage 3 PRRSV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete animal-virus origin and dispersal application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic analysis to identify the geographic center and spread of HIV-1 in Russia's Krasnoyarsk region.
Subjects influenza N6 genes to phylogeographic analysis to reconstruct inter- and intracontinental dissemination along bird flyways.
Performs phylogeographic analyses to trace HCV subtype origins and statistically supported dispersal pathways involving Cyprus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C virus dispersal application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and Bayesian analyses with geographic distributions to compare the phylogeographical histories of two M. tuberculosis lineages.
Uses Bayesian phylogeographic analyses to reconstruct early HIV-1 expansion and bidirectional transmission across Hispaniola.
Conducts phylogeographic characterization of PCV2 sequences to assess genotype history and geographic circulation in Taiwan.
Presents a genome-based phylogeographical analysis of global gonococcal isolates to infer origin and dissemination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete bacterial-pathogen origin and global-spread application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian continuous-spread reconstruction to compare rice yellow mottle virus phylogeography in Madagascar and continental Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete plant-virus invasion and dispersal application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines surveillance-linked sequences with phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses to characterize hantavirus endemic areas in Germany.
Uses phylogeographic analysis to estimate the origins, directions, and timing of Lassa-virus lineage dispersal within Nigeria.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Lassa-virus geographic and temporal reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome SNP typing and spatial case data to investigate the phylogeography of a passerine-associated Salmonella Typhimurium variant.
Performs whole-genome phylogeographic reconstruction to identify West Nile virus migration routes and introductions in Europe.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete West Nile virus transmission application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents, simulation-tests, and applies a new approach linking environmental conditions to phylogeographic lineage-dispersal direction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development with simulation-based performance assessment and a rabies application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a phylogeographic population study of Aedes aegypti to infer its origin and invasion history in Cape Verde.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The focal lineage is a mosquito vector rather than an infectious agent.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses historical Yersinia pestis genomes to reconstruct the geographic entry and subsequent diversification of second-pandemic plague lineages.
Reconstructs phylogeographic dynamics of an HIV-1 lineage to infer introductions and dissemination across northern Brazil.
Performs time-calibrated phylogeographic analysis of Lassa-virus genomes to infer entry into Liberia and regional spread.
Uses Bayesian phylogeographic reconstructions to identify bidirectional rabies-virus dispersal and recurrent extinction-recolonization in Costa Rica.
Uses global-scale phylogeographic analyses of a tomato pathogen to infer multiple introductions into Iran.
Uses a Bayesian discrete phylogeographic approach to infer origins and dispersion networks of influenza B lineages in China.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian Markov chain Monte Carlo phylogeographic discrete approach

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian evolutionary analysis to reconstruct the continental dispersion of hepatitis delta virus genotype 3.
Uses a Bayesian coalescent and phylogeographic framework to reconstruct the introduction and diversification of HCV-2b in Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C virus spatiotemporal reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian sequence analysis in an explicitly phylogeographical study to narrow the estimated timing and source context of Zika introduction into Brazil.
Performs phylogeographic analysis of Zika genomes to estimate introductions, cryptic circulation, and cross-border movement involving Colombia.
Introduces a method for heterogeneous geographic sampling priors in continuous phylogeographic inference and applies it to H5N1.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The new method is explicitly implemented in the BEAST 1.10 software package.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Investigates the worldwide phylogeography and evolutionary history of Culex pipiens densovirus using isolates from laboratory and natural mosquito populations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to a mosquito-infecting virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs Bayesian phylogeographic analyses on global hRSV ON1 sequences to infer origin and spread.
Performs phylogeographic analysis of KFDV whole genomes and E-gene sequences to infer regional evolution and dispersal.
Develops and validates a parsimonious phylogeographic reconstruction method using air-network effective distances, then applies it to pandemic H1N1.
Evaluates large-scale Bayesian phylogeography across HIV datasets and identifies interpretability limits caused by weak phylogenetic structure and tree uncertainty.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and limitation assessment of large-scale phylogeographic inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs preliminary phylogeographic analysis and Bayesian reconstruction of 2019-nCoV origins and international transmission.
Combines whole-genome sequencing and spatial scan statistics in phylogeographic analysis of M. ulcerans genotypes and reservoirs in Benin.
Investigates tsetse-fly phylogeography using microsatellite data to infer population connectivity and dispersal barriers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The focal analysis concerns vector population structure rather than pathogen genomic diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic analysis to infer HIV-1 lineage emergence in Nigerian cities and migration toward rural areas.
Uses tick mitochondrial whole-genome phylogeny to evaluate phylogeographic structure across Norway and Europe.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The focal phylogeographic structure is that of the tick vector, not TBEV diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs Bayesian phylogeographic analysis of chikungunya genomes to reconstruct evolution and international exportations from India.
Investigates the phylogeography of clinical and aquatic V. cholerae isolates to infer reservoir establishment and transmission across environments.
Conducts Bayesian discrete phylogeographic analysis to reconstruct H9N2 origins, spread, and transmission routes involving Egypt.
Investigates the evolutionary history and intercontinental gene flow of H13 and H16 avian influenza viruses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete viral phylogeography and gene-flow application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Predicts viral sharing from host phylogenetic similarity and geographic range overlap; it does not reconstruct pathogen lineage geography or genomic diffusion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a macroecological viral-sharing model rather than a lineage source for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian space-diffusion and phylogeographic analyses to infer host-specific spread hubs of H5 avian influenza in China.
Performs a phylogeographic analysis of feline-coronavirus sequences to reconstruct origin and dispersion through Europe.
Uses phylogeographic investigation of full genomes to identify porcine-deltacoronavirus transmission and long-distance dispersal in China.
Uses temporal and discrete-trait Bayesian phylogeography to compare MERS-CoV transmission routes in hospital and sporadic cases.
Uses genomic phylogeographic evidence to infer the timing and likely source of Zika-virus introduction into Cape Verde.
Applies phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses to viral genomes to identify source countries and circulation periods for respiratory-virus lineages in Vietnam.
Uses phylogeographical analysis to identify intercontinental transmission and within-country circulation of A. johnsonii lineages.
Provides GeoBoost2, a location-extraction and metadata-enrichment pipeline designed to support virus phylogeography.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit web application and source-code implementation for phylogeographic metadata preparation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a geographically representative SARS-CoV-2 genomic dataset to estimate introductions, local spread, and exportations across Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of viral dispersal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes geographically distributed SARS-CoV-2 genomes to infer international transmission patterns and viral geographic structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete comparative-genomic investigation of viral geographic spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces scalable methods for simulating spherical Brownian motion on timetrees and estimating global diffusion coefficients from tip coordinates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The new phylogeographic estimation and simulation methods are explicitly implemented in the R package castor.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies ancestral-state reconstruction to sequence data to study the phylogeography and global evolutionary history of V. vulnificus.
Simulation-evaluates Bayesian phylogeographic root-state accuracy and demonstrates limitations of KL divergence as a performance metric.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of phylogeographic model performance under varying dataset and state-space conditions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses whether the mgc2 gene accurately supports phylogeographic reconstruction while also tracing M. gallisepticum expansion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of a marker's accuracy for phylogeographic evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Qualitatively evaluates feasibility, interpretation, utility, and implementation barriers for phylogeographic and phylodynamic HIV-surveillance tools.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit needs assessment identifying methodological and implementation limitations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic analysis of HHV-8 sequences to identify likely geographic sources and links with human ancestry in Argentina.
Uses viral phylogeographic structure and inference to evaluate how urban barriers shape FIV transmission among bobcat populations.
Performs phylogeographic analysis of more than 41,000 SARS-CoV-2 genomes to reconstruct introductions and within-Spain transmission patterns.
Uses discrete phylogeographic analysis with a generalized linear model to reconstruct and compare interprovincial spread of five HIV types.
Uses genome sequences to reconstruct the phylogeographic and demographic dynamics of MRSA ST228/ST111 across Europe.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete MRSA lineage-origin and dissemination application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographical analysis to infer Newcastle-disease-virus migration hubs, diffusion regions, and host-directional spread.
Reviews phylogeographic theory, methods, applications, power, and pitfalls for studying plant-pathogen emergence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological review intended to guide selection and interpretation of phylogeographic approaches.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome phylogenetic and geographic analyses to build a phylogeographic map of Hantaan-virus diversity and contact zones.
Conducts comprehensive phylogeographical analysis to compare genetic and geographic structure of Baltic-group tick-borne encephalitis viruses.
Performs phylogeographic analysis showing regional spread of a pneumococcal serotype 12F cluster from Kanto.
Uses phylogeographic analyses of HIV sequences to reconstruct local and cross-border viral migration around San Diego and Tijuana.
Uses Bayesian discrete phylogeography to infer strongly supported bidirectional H5N1 migration between Cambodia and Vietnam.
Reconstructs EV-A71 migration in China through phylogeographical analysis and geographic ancestral reconstruction.
Presents a spatially explicit phylogeographic-phylodynamic framework and analytical workflow for formal epidemiological hypothesis testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework illustrated with West Nile virus genomes and simulation contrasts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic analysis of SARS-CoV-2 genomes to characterize the geographic distributions of Chicago viral clades.
Uses whole-genome phylogeographic analysis of newly enriched dolphin-morbillivirus sequences to distinguish regional epidemic strains.
Performs phylogeographic analysis of SARS-CoV-2 genomes to infer introductions and transmission clusters in Uruguay.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 introduction and local-transmission application.
